XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable

Indicates whether the calling title has new content offerings available. This function can be called without first logging onto the Xbox Live service, and can thus be called by titles that are using the Content Download Manager.

HRESULT XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable(
    DWORD dwBitFilter,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwBitFilter
[in] The offering filter flags. Only content offerings that have one or more of these title-specific bits set will be checked.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to an event that will be signaled when there is work to be done on this task. This parameter can be NULL if no such event is required.
phTask
[out] Pointer to a variable of type XONLINETASK_HANDLE that receives a task handle representing this request. The handle can then be used in calls to XOnlineTaskContinue to perform work associated with this task.

Return Values

If the function successfully creates the asynchronous task, the return value is S_OK.

Remarks

In order to initialize the network layer, the XOnlineStartup function must be called prior to calling XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable. However, XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable can be called without first calling XOnlineLogon to log onto the Xbox Live service; if the title is not already logged on, the necessary logon (and logoff) steps will be performed internally.

XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable creates an asynchronous task and returns a handle to that task in the phTask parameter. In order to perform work on (and eventually complete) this asynchronous task, repeatedly call XOnlineTaskContinue with the task handle until that function returns a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING.

The return value from XOnlineTaskContinue indicates the results of the query:

Value Description
XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING The task is still processing the request. Keep calling XOnlineTaskContinue.
XONLINE_S_OFFERING_NEW_CONTENT New content matching the specified bit flags is available.
XONLINE_S_OFFERING_NO_NEW_CONTENT No new content matching the specified bit flags is available.
An error return value (XONLINE_E_xxx) An error occurred while processing the request. Generally, simply treat an error as an indication that no new content is available. For debugging purposes, or for purposes of integrated error handling for a full-function Xbox Live title, possible error return values include those that can be returned by a failed XOnlineLogon call. See the XOnlineLogon Return Values for more information.

After the task has completed, call XOnlineTaskClose with the task handle in order to free associated resources.

The hWorkEvent parameter can be used to pass an event handle to the enumeration function. That event handle will be signaled when there is work to be performed on the asynchronous task. A secondary thread could be waiting on this event handle to know when asynchronous task work needs to be done, and call XOnlineTaskContinue to do so. Thus, the secondary thread can avoid spinning on calls to XOnlineTaskContinue when no work needs to be done.

The definition of the term "new content" is content that the player has not previously seen. Therefore, this function returns XONLINE_S_OFFERING_NO_NEW_CONTENT when there is content that the player has previously seen but not downloaded.

The recommended use for this function is to determine quickly whether new content is available, and to indicate that availability to the user in some appropriate way. However, titles should not prevent users from viewing or downloading available content when XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable indicates that no new content is available; users may wish to browse or download previous (possibly unpurchased) content.

Note    XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable checks against a single region within the Xbox's region code, rather than each individual country. Thus, the title will receive false positives unless new content is offered to every country in a given region at one time.

Note that some caching is done when determining whether new content is available. It may be up to three hours between the time that new content is posted to a server and the time that titles will see the content as available.

Titles using the Content Download Manager must link with the online libraries in order to call XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib (xonlines.lib for final testing and certification).

See Also

Xbox Live Functions, Content Download Manager, Finding Content | XOnlineTaskContinue, XOnlineTaskClose, XOnlineOfferingEnumerate
